methods.description: Process for collecting airborne pollen data using a volumetric spore trap at a specific location and height over a defined period.

steps.description: The research was conducted by means of the volumetric method using a Burkard-type spore trap which belongs to the Allergen Research Center.
steps.searchable_summary: Deploy and operate a Burkard-type spore trap using the volumetric method.
outputs: Raw air sample data (pollen captured on sample medium)

steps.description: The sampling site was located at a height of approximately 20 metres above the ground on the premises of the Faculty of Earth Sciences at the University of Silesia in the northern part of Pogoń -a district of Sosnowiec -with well-spaced residential blocks of flats.The geographic coordinates for the sampling site are as follows: 50 o 17 ' 50"N and 19° 08' 20"E.
steps.searchable_summary: Document the specific location and height of the pollen sampling site.

methods.description: Microscopic examination of collected pollen samples to identify and count pollen grains, followed by conversion to daily concentration.

steps.description: A microscopic analysis of hazel and alder pollen, after preparations had been stained with alkaline fuchsin, was performed...
steps.searchable_summary: Stain pollen sample preparations with alkaline fuchsin.
inputs: Raw pollen sample medium (e.g., tape segments)

steps.description: A microscopic analysis of hazel and alder pollen... was performed on the surface of 4 horizontal strips (M a n d r i o l i et al. 1998).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform microscopic analysis and count pollen grains on specified areas (4 horizontal strips).
outputs: Raw counts of pollen grains per analyzed area

steps.description: The result was expressed as the average daily number of pollen grains per cubic metre of air.
steps.searchable_summary: Convert raw pollen counts to average daily concentration per cubic metre of air.

methods.description: Method to define the start and end dates of a pollen season based on reaching specific percentage thresholds (1% and 99%) of the cumulative annual pollen count.

steps.description: The duration of pollen seasons was determined by means of the 98% method, according to which the beginning of the season was on the day when 1% of the cumulative sum of a given taxon pollen was recorded and the end when 99% of pollen was found (E m b e r l i n et al. 1994; S p i e k s m a and N i k k e l s , 1998).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the cumulative sum of daily pollen counts for a given taxon over the year.
inputs: Daily pollen concentrations for a taxon over a year

steps.description: according to which the beginning of the season was on the day when 1% of the cumulative sum of a given taxon pollen was recorded...
steps.searchable_summary: Identify the date when the cumulative pollen sum reaches 1% of the total annual sum (beginning of season).

steps.description: ...and the end when 99% of pollen was found (E m b e r l i n et al. 1994; S p i e k s m a and N i k k e l s , 1998).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify the date when the cumulative pollen sum reaches 99% of the total annual sum (end of season).

methods.description: Method to determine specific dates within a pollen season based on reaching certain percentage thresholds (1%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 99%) of the total annual pollen count.

steps.description: The dates of successive stages of the pollen season were determined as the next day of the year on which the accumulated number of pollen grains from the beginning of the year reached a specified percentage value of all the grains in a given year...
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the accumulated daily pollen count from the beginning of the year for a given taxon.
inputs: Daily pollen concentrations for a taxon over a year

steps.searchable_summary: Identify the date when the accumulated pollen count reaches 1% of the total annual count.
Identify the date when the accumulated pollen count reaches 25% of the total annual count.
Identify the date when the accumulated pollen count reaches 50% of the total annual count.
Identify the date when the accumulated pollen count reaches 75% of the total annual count.
Identify the date when the accumulated pollen count reaches 99% of the total annual count.

methods.description: Using non-hierarchical k-means clustering to classify the course of pollen seasons based on their characteristics.

steps.description: Cluster analysis was used to classify the course of pollen seasons, including non-hierarchical clustering of multi-feature objects -k-means clustering.
steps.searchable_summary: Select k-means clustering for non-hierarchical grouping of pollen season data.

steps.description: This method is based on the automatic clustering of objects that are similar to each other. The k-means method is used to analyze large amounts of data...
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare pollen season data as multi-feature objects suitable for k-means clustering.
inputs: Data describing the course/characteristics of pollen seasons (e.g., daily counts, season dates, peak values, etc.)
outputs: Feature vectors or data matrix for clustering

steps.description: In this method, the number of clusters is imposed by the researcher, so this is a subjective method. In this study, pollen periods were divided into five groups according to the author, because such a division best showed differences among individual groups.
steps.searchable_summary: Specify the number of clusters (k) for the k-means algorithm (k=5).
inputs: Researcher's decision on number of clusters (k=5)

steps.description: Cluster analysis was used to classify the course of pollen seasons, including non-hierarchical clustering of multi-feature objects -k-means clustering.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply the k-means clustering algorithm to the pollen season data.
outputs: Cluster assignments for each pollen season

steps.description: The k-means method is used to analyze large amounts of data, and its essence is to reduce the accumulated information into several basic categories, which makes it easy to understand the relevant phenomena and to draw general conclusions.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze and interpret the characteristics of the resulting pollen season clusters.
outputs: Description and interpretation of cluster characteristics
